rationale: The cited paper is used for core building blocks, not just background. The citing article explicitly relies on Fader, Hardie, and Lee (2005b) for the DERT concept and for iso-value-curve style analysis, and it compares its own backward-bending contour results to that prior work. The new model adds transaction-specific attributes and incremental DERT within the same customer-base valuation tradition, indicating a direct continuation of the cited framework.
relation_description: The citing paper extends Fader, Hardie, and Lee (2005b) by carrying its recency-frequency valuation and iso-value-curve logic into a richer latent attrition model with transaction-level covariates. It generalizes the prior customer-base analysis to study how service-attribute shocks change expected residual value.
